data.body:
PASS-1 PLAN REVIEW — criterion A1 (anti-slop / NIH / over-engineering). One finding (NIH / rebuilds-existing) plus affirmations. Findings are grounded checklist items the author should resolve before this doc plan proceeds.

AFFIRMATIONS (A1 sub-checks that PASS):
- YAGNI: the doc serves a current done-definition (the plan-review epic's "Docs" AC + the "reuse rebar.llm extension points" ACs), not a hypothetical — affirmed.
- No config-surface proliferation: documentation-only, zero new config keys — affirmed.
- No golden-hammer / cargo-cult / resume-driven / premature-optimization / premature-abstraction: no new abstraction, dependency, or tech is introduced; it documents existing surfaces — affirmed.

FINDING (A1 — NIH / rebuilds functionality already in the codebase):

- [ ] Confirm whether the proposed "new doc (e.g. `docs/reuse-surface.md`)" is genuinely new or a rebuild of an existing doc, and re-scope from "create" to "verify/extend the gaps" — because `docs/reuse-surface.md` ALREADY EXISTS and already delivers every AC of this plan.
  - location: `docs/reuse-surface.md` (whole file); cross-links at `CLAUDE.md:8`, `docs/llm-framework.md:443`; output-schema half at `docs/output-schemas.md`.
  - criteria: A1
  - evidence:
    - The plan names its own target: AC1 "new doc under `docs/`, e.g. `docs/reuse-surface.md`". That exact path is present and is titled "Reusable machinery — developer API reference", covering §1 signing / §2 LLM runtime / §3 prompt library / §4 output-schema seam — the same four sections this plan enumerates.
    - AC2 (signing: manifest format, HMAC/PAYLOAD_VERSION canonicalisation, env-key custody, certified|mismatch|foreign_key|unsigned verdict vocab, head_sha staleness binding, worked NEW-gate example) is already satisfied: `docs/reuse-surface.md` §1 documents `sign_manifest`/`verify_signature`/`verify_record`/`signing_key`/`key_fingerprint`/`compute_signature`/`parse_manifest`/`head_sha`, the verdict table, last-writer-wins SIGNATURE replay, AND a "Worked example — a NEW gate reusing the surface" block (lines ~101-122) that is exactly the "not just the close gate" example AC2 asks for.
    - AC3 (Runner protocol, every RunRequest field, structured-output path, run_workflow incl. parts unused by the epic): already in §2 — `RunRequest` with every field (mode/execution_mode/output_schema/extra_tools/…), the `Runner` protocol (run/preflight), `get_runner`, PydanticAIRunner/FakeRunner, and `run_workflow(...)` with step kinds + when-to-use guidance.
    - AC4 (get_prompt/resolve_prompt, front-matter closed-key contract, `.rebar/prompts/` override resolution, adding a reviewer/prompt): already in §3, including the closed `FRONT_MATTER_KEYS` set, override/variant resolution table, the derived index + CI drift/parity/canonical gates, and a step-by-step "Adding a prompt".
    - AC4/output-schema seam: §4 of `reuse-surface.md` plus a dedicated `docs/output-schemas.md` already document `register_contract`/`response_model_for`, `validate_structured`, packaged `*.schema.json`, `OUTPUT_SCHEMAS`, and the coverage guards.
    - AC5 (human + LLM audience, signatures + copy-pasteable examples): the existing doc's header explicitly states "Audience: human developers and LLM agents" and is full of signatures + runnable code blocks.
    - AC6 (cross-linked from `docs/llm-framework.md` AND listed in `CLAUDE.md`): already done — `docs/llm-framework.md:443` ("See also — reuse reference") and `CLAUDE.md:8` ("reusable-machinery API reference `docs/reuse-surface.md`"). Note `CLAUDE.md` already lists it, so the AC6 second clause is met today.
    - It is already consumed/cited by production code: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/registry.py:41` and `docs/plan-review-gate.md` (lines 18/67/127) point at `reuse-surface.md` §3 / the signing surface — so this is production documentation, NOT a docs/experiments reference-to-rebuild (FP7 anti-FP checked and does not apply: the existing file is under `docs/`, not `docs/experiments/`, and is not a POC).
  - suggested_fix: Re-scope this ticket from "create a new reference" to "AUDIT `docs/reuse-surface.md` (+ `docs/output-schemas.md`) against the current source for drift and fill any genuinely-missing AC item, then close." If the audit finds the existing doc fully covers AC1-AC6 (it appears to), state that and close as already-satisfied rather than authoring a duplicate. If the intent is a DIFFERENT, additional doc, the Why/Scope must name what `docs/reuse-surface.md` is missing — as written, the scope is indistinguishable from the existing file and would rebuild it.

Note for AC7 ("verified against current source — no drift"): this is the one part NOT already obviously done and is the legitimately-valuable residual — a drift audit of the existing doc's signatures vs. `src/rebar/signing.py`, `rebar.llm.runner`, `rebar.llm.workflow`, `rebar.llm.prompts`, `rebar.llm.contracts`, `rebar.llm.findings`. Recommend the re-scoped ticket center on THAT verification rather than fresh authorship.
